claim: High levels of copeptin increase risk of diabetes.

result.claim: High levels of copeptin increase risk of diabetes.
result.reasoning: High levels of copeptin are associated with an increased risk of developing diabetes, particularly in women. A cohort study (Paper ID: 5500802) with a sample size of 7972 individuals and a 7.7-year follow-up found that the association of plasma copeptin with the risk of future diabetes was stronger in women (OR 1.49, 95% CI [1.24, 1.79]) than in men (OR 1.01, 95% CI [0.85, 1.19]). This study has a credibility rating of 2.3/5. The additive value of copeptin in women for predicting diabetes risk was maintained even when other independent predictors like glucose were included in the model.

evidence.text: The additive value of copeptin in women was maintained when glucose was included in the model.
evidence.source: Sex differences in the association between plasma copeptin and incident type 2 diabetes: the Prevention of Renal and Vascular Endstage Disease (PREVEND) study

evidence.text: Admission copeptin levels were associated with 10-year all-cause mortality with an age-adjusted hazard ratio of 1.7.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: Admission and day 7 copeptin levels were strong predictors for adverse outcome.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: J. Odermatt, R. Bolliger, L. Hersberger, M. Ottiger, M. Christ-Crain, B. Mueller, P. Schuetz studied copeptin.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: In women, the odds ratio (OR) of future diabetes associated with copeptin was 1.49, with a 95% confidence interval (CI) of [1.24, 1.79].
evidence.source: Sex differences in the association between plasma copeptin and incident type 2 diabetes: the Prevention of Renal and Vascular Endstage Disease (PREVEND) study

evidence.text: Elevated copeptin levels (≥5.6 pmol/L) in patients with T2DM were independently associated with an increased risk of MACE.
evidence.source: Plasma copeptin independently predicts cardiovascular events but not all-cause mortality in patients with type 2 diabetes: a prospective observational study.

evidence.text: Admission copeptin levels were associated with 10-year all-cause mortality with an age-adjusted hazard ratio of 1.7 (95%CI, 1.2-2.5).
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: Water supplementation in habitual low drinkers with high copeptin may reduce fasting glucose or glucagon.
evidence.source: A Journey through the Early Evidence Linking Hydration to Metabolic Health.
